title: Flueggea bailloniana
taxonomy: Flueggea bailloniana is a member of the family Phyllanthaceae, which is a family of flowering plants native to tropical regions. This plant is classified as a genus within the family, and it is closely related to other genera such as Phyllanthus and Breynia. Flueggea bailloniana is a species of flowering plant that is native to Madagascar. The classification of Flueggea bailloniana is based on its physical characteristics, including its leaf shape and flower structure. The plant's classification also reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Phyllanthaceae family.

intro: Flueggea bailloniana, also known as the Indian soap tree or Japa Pushpi, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and distinctive, soap-like bark. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress.
